events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to assess a fall protection setup. The scenario: worker at 15 feet on steel deck, full-body harness, clean unfrayed webbing, fold-out impact indicator tag intact, inspection grid punched for current quarter. Lanyard snaps to engineered anchor strap around structural beam marked with rated-anchor tag, connected at shoulder height. No defects mentioned. All seems compliant. Need to output JSON with decision, risk, work_stage, component_conditions, percent_complete, value, sound_source, confidence, event_time, rate, order, workable, findings, actions, rationale, references. We need to check if any deficiency. The setup appears proper: harness inspected, anchor rated, lanyard attached at shoulder height (good, not too low). Distance: 15 feet. Need to consider if there's any issue like free fall distance, swing fall, etc. Not mentioned. So likely pass. Decision: pass. Risk: low (compliant). Work stage: in-progress (worker is actively on deck). Component conditions: none, so empty list. Percent complete: work is in-progress, but what's the overall progress? Not specified, but maybe we can estimate? The task is just inspecting fall protection, not the whole project. The scenario says "Stage of the work when observed: in-progress". So percent complete might be unknown, but we can estimate 50%? The instruction says: "percent_complete is your numeric estimate of overall progress". Since we only see fall protection setup, maybe the overall work is the deck installation? Not clear. We can set it to 50 as a neutral estimate, or maybe 0? The worker is on the deck, work is in-progress, so maybe 50. But it's not specified. I'll set 50. value: no numeric reading asked, so 0. sound_source: not asked, empty string. confidence: high, maybe 0.95. event_time, rate: not relevant, 0. order and workable: not asked, empty lists. findings: no defects, so empty. actions: none, empty. rationale: "Harness is inspected, anchor is rated, lanyard connected at shoulder height, no visible defects." references: ["OSHA 1926.502"]. So I'll output JSON accordingly.

}
